title: Sfn timing for frequency hopping systems
abstract: According to an aspect, a wireless device identifies system frame numbers (SFNs) that are used for wireless transmissions that use a broadcast channel (BCH) transmission time interval (TTI), where frequency hopping cycles (FHCs) are not aligned with a cycle of the SFNs. The wireless device receives FH information indicating how the FHC used for wireless transmissions relates to the SFN cycle and identifies an SFN timing for the wireless transmissions based on the FH information.
claims:
Claims 1. A method (500) in a network node (30) for identifying system frame numbers, SFNs, that are used for wireless transmissions that use a broadcast channel, BCH, transmission time interval, TTI, wherein frequency hopping cycles, FHCs, are not aligned with a cycle of the SFNs, the method (500) comprising: determining (502) FH information indicating how the FHC used for wireless transmissions relates to the SFN cycle, such that a wireless device (50) is able to use the FH information to identify an SFN timing for the wireless transmissions; and transmitting (504) the FH information to a wireless device (50). 2. The method (500) of claim 1, wherein the SFN timing is used to determine a specific frequency hop within the FHC. 3. The method (500) of claim 1 or 2, wherein the SFN timing is identified based on a number of frequency hops in the FHC, a dwell time for each hopping frequency, and a number of SFNs in the SFN cycle. 4. The method (500) of any of claims 1-3, wherein determining (502) the FH information comprises determining an FHC cycle number, FHCN, identifying the FHC to be used, such that the FHCN may be used by the wireless device (50) to identify the SFN timing, and wherein transmitting the FH information to the wireless device (50) comprises transmitting the FHCN. 5. The method (500) of claim 4, wherein the SFN timing is identified according to: SFN = (thop Â· T dwell/ 10) mod SFN_cycle, where t_h op = (FHCN Â· N), and where N is the number of hops in the FHC. 6. The method (500) of claim 4 or 5, wherein the FHCN is transmitted without any accompanying SFN identifier. 7. The method (500) of any of claims 4-6, wherein the FHCN is transmitted in a master information block, MIB. 8. The method (500) of claim 7, wherein the MIB is transmitted in one dwell of the FHC. 9. The method (500) of any of claims 4-8, wherein determining the FHCN comprises deriving the FF1C pseudo-randomly from a physical cell identity, PCID, and/or a network identifier, NetID, associated with the network node (30). 10. The method (500) of any of claims 1-9, wherein the number of bits used for signaling the FF1C depends on a duration of the FF1C and a duration of the dwell. 11. The method (500) of any of claims 1-10, wherein the wireless transmissions use a narrow band, NB, physical broadcast channel, NPBCF1 TTI. 12. The method (500) of any of claims 1-11, wherein the TTI of the BCF1 is divided into a plurality of blocks, each block comprising a plurality of radio frames and carrying a code subblock, CSB, version that is transmitted in one or more subframes in one or more radio frames, wherein different CSB versions are used in successive blocks within the TTIs, and wherein determining (502) the FF1 information comprises determining the FF1 information based on the wireless device (50) being able to use the CSB version to help identify a block, the SFN timing, and/or the FF1C for the TTI. 13. The method (500) of claim 12, further comprising transmitting the FF1 information with fewer bits than if the CSB version was not used, wherein the number of the fewer bits is based on the total number of CSB versions. 14. The method (500) of claim 12 or 13, wherein the one or more subframes are one or more specific subframes within a radio frame or multiple consecutive subframes. 15. A method (800), in a wireless device (50), for identifying system frame numbers, SFNs, that are used for wireless transmissions that use a broadcast channel, BCF1, transmission time interval, TTI, wherein frequency hopping cycles, FFlCs, are not aligned with a cycle of the SFNs, the method (600) comprising: receiving (802) FF1 information indicating how the FF1C used for wireless transmissions relates to the SFN cycle; and 

 identifying (804) an SFN timing for the wireless transmissions based on the FH information. 16. The method (800) of claim 15, further comprising using the SFN timing to determine a specific frequency hop within the FF1C. 17. The method (800) of claim 15 or 16, wherein identifying the SFN timing comprises identifying the SFN timing based on a number of frequency hops in the FF1C, a dwell time for each hopping frequency, and a number of the SFNs in the SFN cycle. 18. The method (800) of any of claims 15-17, wherein receiving the FF1 information comprises receiving an FF1C number, FF1CN, identifying the FF1C to be used, and wherein the SFN timing is based on the FF1CN. 19. The method (800) of claim 18, wherein identifying the SFN timing comprises identifying the SFN timing according to: SFN = (thop Â· T dwell/ 10) mod SFN_cycle, where t_ hop = (FF1CN Â· N), and where N is the number of hops in the FF1C. 20. The method (800) of claim 18 or 19, wherein the FF1CN is determined by deriving the FF1C pseudo-randomly from a physical cell identity, PCID, and/or a network identifier, NetID, associated with a network node (30). 21. The method (800) of any of claims 15-20, wherein the wireless transmissions use a narrow band, NB, physical broadcast channel, NPBCF1 TTI. 22. The method (800) of any of claims 15-21, wherein the TTI is divided into a plurality of blocks, each block comprising a plurality of radio frames and carrying a code subblock, CSB, version that is transmitted in one or more subframes in one or more radio frames, wherein different CSB versions are used in successive blocks within the TTIs, and wherein identifying (804) the SFN timing comprises using the CSB version to help identify a block, the SFN timing, and/or the FF1C for the TTI. 23. The method (800) of claim 22, further comprising, in response to using a CSB version to 

 help identify the FHC, identifying the FHC with fewer bits than if the CSB version was not used, wherein the number of the fewer bits is based on a total number of CSB. 24. The method (800) of claim 22 or 23, wherein the one or more subframes are one or more specific subframes within a radio frame or multiple consecutive subframes. 25. The method (800) of any of claims 18-24, wherein the FF1CN is received in a master information block, MIB. 26. The method (800) of claim 25, wherein the MIB is acquired in one dwell of the FF1C. 27. A network node (30) configured for identifying system frame numbers, SFNs, that are used for wireless transmissions that use a broadcast channel, BCF1, transmission time interval, TTI, wherein frequency hopping cycles, FFICs, are not aligned with a cycle of the SFNs, the network node (30) comprising: transceiver circuitry (36) configured for communicating with wireless devices operating in a wireless communication network; and processing circuitry (32) operatively associated with the transceiver circuitry (36) and configured to: determine FF1 information indicating how the FF1C used for wireless transmissions relates to the SFN cycle, such that a wireless device (50) is able to use the FF1 information to identify an SFN timing for the wireless transmissions; and transmit the FF1 information to a wireless device (50). 28. The network node (30) of claim 27, wherein the processing circuitry (32) is configured to use the SFN timing to determine a specific frequency hop within the FF1C. 29. The network node (30) of claim 27 or 28, wherein the processing circuitry (32) is configured to identify the SFN timing based on a number of frequency hops in the FF1C, a dwell time for each hopping frequency and the number of SFNs in the SFN cycle. 30. The network node (30) of any of claims 27-29, wherein the processing circuitry (32) is configured to determine the FF1 information by determining an FF1C cycle number, FF1CN, 

 identifying the FHC to be used, such that the FHCN may be used by the wireless device (50) to identify the SFN timing, and wherein the processing circuitry (32) is configured to transmit the FF1 information to the wireless device (50) by transmitting the FF1CN. 31. The network node (30) of claim 30, wherein the processing circuitry (32) is configured to identify the SFN timing according to: SFN = (thop Â· T dwell/ 10) mod SFN_cycle, where t_h op = (FF1CN Â· N), and where N is the number of hops in the FF1C. 32. The network node (30) of claim 31, wherein the FF1CN is transmitted without any accompanying SFN identifier. 33. The network node (30) of any of claims 30-32, wherein the FF1CN is transmitted in a master information block, MIB. 34. The network node (30) of claim 33, wherein the MIB is transmitted in one dwell of the FHC. 35. The network node (30) of any of claims 30-34, wherein the processing circuitry (32) is configured to determine the FHCN by deriving the FHC pseudo-randomly from a physical cell identity, PCID, and/or a network identifier, NetID, associated with the network node (30). 36. The network node (30) of any of claims 27-35, wherein a number of bits used for signaling the FHC depends on a duration of the FHC and a duration of the dwell. 37. The network node (30) of any of claims 27-36, wherein the wireless transmissions use a narrow band, NB, physical broadcast channel, NPBCH TTI. 38. The network node (30) of any of claims 27-37, wherein the TTI is divided into a plurality of blocks, each block comprising a plurality of radio frames and carrying a code subblock, CSB, version that is transmitted in one or more subframes in one or more radio frames, wherein different CSB versions are used in successive blocks within the TTIs, and wherein the processing circuitry (32) is configured to determine the FH information based on the 

 wireless device (50) being able to use the CSB version to help identify a block, the SFN timing, and/or the FHC for the TTI. 39. The network node (30) of claim 38, wherein the processing circuitry (32) is configured to transmit the FF1 information with fewer bits than if the CSB version was not used, wherein the number of the fewer bits is based on a total number of CSB versions. 40. The network node (30) of any of claim 38 or 39, wherein the one or more subframes are specific subframes within a radio frame or multiple consecutive subframes. 41. A wireless device (50) configured for identifying system frame numbers, SFNs, that are used for wireless transmissions that use a broadcast channel, BCF1, transmission time interval, TTI, wherein frequency hopping cycles, FFICs, are not aligned with a cycle of the SFNs, the wireless device (50) comprising: transceiver circuitry (56) configured for communicating with network nodes (30) in a wireless communication network; and processing circuitry (52) operatively associated with the transceiver circuitry (56) and configured to: receive FF1 information indicating how the FF1C used for wireless transmissions relates to the SFN cycle; and identify an SFN timing for the wireless transmissions based on the FF1 information. 42. The wireless device (50) of claim 41, wherein the processing circuitry (52) is configured to use the SFN timing to determine a specific frequency hop within the FF1C. 43. The wireless device (50) of claim 41 or 42, wherein the processing circuitry (52) is configured to identify the SFN timing by identifying the SFN timing based on a number of frequency hops in the FF1C, a dwell time for each hopping frequency and the number of SFNs in the SFN cycle. 44. The wireless device (50) of any of claims 41-43, wherein the processing circuitry (52) is configured to receive the FF1 information by receiving an FF1C number, FF1CN, identifying the FF1C to be used, and wherein the SFN timing is based on the FF1CN. 45. The wireless device (50) of claim 44, wherein the processing circuitry (52) is configured to identify the SFN timing by identifying the SFN timing according to: SFN = (t hop Â· T dwell / 10) mod SFN_cycle, where t hop = (FF1CN Â· N), and FF1CN is the number of hops in the FHC. 46. The wireless device (50) of any of claims 34-38, wherein the wireless transmissions use a narrow band, NB, physical broadcast channel, NPBCH TTI. 47. The wireless device (50) of any of claims 41-46, wherein the TTI is divided into a plurality of blocks, each block comprising a plurality of radio frames and carrying a code subblock, CSB, version that is transmitted in one or more subframes in one or more radio frames, wherein different CSB versions are used in successive blocks within the TTIs, and wherein the processing circuitry (52) is configured to use the CSB version to help identify a block, the SFN timing, and/or the FF1C for the TTI. 48. The wireless device (50) of claim 47, wherein the processing circuitry (52) is configured to, in response to using a CSB version to help identify the FF1C, identify the FF1C with fewer bits than if the CSB version was not used, wherein the number of fewer bits is based on the total number of CSB. 49. The wireless device (50) of any of claim 47 or 48, wherein the one or more subframes are one or more specific subframes within a radio frame or multiple subframes are consecutive subframes. 50. The wireless device (50) of any of claims 41-49, wherein the FF1CN is received in a master information block, MTB. 51. The wireless device (50) of claim 50, wherein the MIB is acquired in one dwell of the FHC. 52. The wireless device (50) of any of claims 44-51, wherein the FHCN is determined by deriving the FHC pseudo-randomly from a physical cell identity, PCID, and/or a network identifier, NetID, associated with the network node (30). 53. A non-transitory, computer-readable medium (44) storing a computer program (46) for identifying system frame numbers, SFNs, that are used for wireless transmissions that use a broadcast channel, BCH, transmission time interval, TTI, wherein frequency hopping cycles, FHCs, are not aligned with a cycle of the SFNs, the computer program (46) comprising instructions that, when executed by a processing circuit (32) of a network node (30) operating in a wireless communication network, cause the network node (30) to: determine FF1 information indicating how the FF1C used for wireless transmissions relates to the SFN cycle, such that a wireless device (50) is able to use the FF1 information to identify an SFN timing for the wireless transmissions; and transmit the FF1 information to a wireless device (50). 54. A non-transitory, computer-readable medium (64) storing a computer program (66) for identifying system frame numbers, SFNs, that are used for wireless transmissions that use a broadcast channel, BCF1, transmission time interval, TTI, wherein frequency hopping cycles, FFlCs, are not aligned with a cycle of the SFNs, the computer program (66) comprising instructions that, when executed by a processing circuit (52) of a wireless device (50) operating in a wireless communication network, cause the wireless device (50) to: receive FF1 information indicating how the FF1C used for wireless transmissions relates to the SFN cycle; and identify an SFN timing for the wireless transmissions based on the FF1 information. 55. A network node (30) adapted to perform a method (500) of any of claims 1-14. 56. A wireless device (50) adapted to perform a method (800) of any of claims 15-26. 57. A computer program product (46, 66), comprising instructions that, when executed on at least one processing circuit (32, 52), cause the at least one processing circuit (32, 52) to carry out a method (500, 800) according to any one of claims 1-26. 58. A carrier containing the computer program product of claim 57, wherein the carrier is one of an electronic signal, optical signal, radio signal, or computer readable storage medium (44, 64). 59. A network node (30) in a wireless communications network, the network node (30) configured for identifying system frame numbers, SFNs, that are used for wireless transmissions that use a broadcast channel, BCH, transmission time interval, TTI, wherein frequency hopping cycles, FHCs, are not aligned with a cycle of the SFNs, the network node (30) comprising: a determining module (2202) for determining FF1 information indicating how the FF1C used for wireless transmissions relates to the SFN cycle, such that a wireless device (50) is able to use the FF1 information to identify an SFN timing for the wireless transmissions; and a transmitting module (2204) for transmitting the FF1 information to a wireless device (50). 60. A wireless device (50) configured for identifying system frame numbers, SFNs, that are used for wireless transmissions that use a broadcast channel, BCF1, transmission time interval, TTI, wherein frequency hopping cycles, FFlCs, are not aligned with a cycle of the SFNs, the wireless device (50) comprising: a receiving module (2202) for receiving FF1 information indicating how the FF1C used for wireless transmissions relates to the SFN cycle; and an identifying module (2204) for identifying an SFN timing for the wireless transmissions based on the FF1 information.
